I just built a CLI tool for Twitter and here's what I learned (thread)
First: the official API v2 is actually great. OAuth 1.0a with PIN-based flow means you can auth entirely from the terminal -- no browser redirects, no callback servers.
Second: threads are just tweets with in_reply_to_tweet_id set to the previous tweet. Post them sequentially with a small delay between each and you're golden.
Third: rate limits on the free tier are tight (50 tweets/day) but totally workable for personal use. The API returns limit headers so you always know where you stand.
The tool is called galah (named after the Australian pink cockatoo). It's open source and built with Node.js. Give it a try if you live in the terminal like I do.
